title: Mexico to file criminal complaints in US over deaths of Mexicans in immigration enforcement
summary: MEXICO CITY, July 9 - Mexico's government plans to file criminal complaints in the U.S. regarding Mexican citizens who died in immigration custody or while being targeted in anti-immigration operations, Foreign Minister Roberto Velasco said on Thursday.
imageAlt: Mexico to file criminal complaints in US over deaths of Mexicans in immigration enforcement

fullArticle:

Protests erupted in Houstan after the shooting of a Mexican national, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, on July 7 by a US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent.

MEXICO CITY – Mexico’s government plans to file criminal complaints in the US regarding Mexican citizens who died in immigration custody or while being targeted in anti-immigration operations, Mexican Foreign Minister Roberto Velasco said on July 9.

Fourteen Mexican nationals have lost their lives while in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, and three more died in arrest operations conducted by the agency, Velasco told a press conference.
